verses.fhb_text: It was now two days before the Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. And the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him.
verses.decision_notes: Pronoun replaced with 'Jesus' at first reference for clarity in a scene where multiple male antecedents follow (FHB-9.13-1).

verses.fhb_text: For they said, "Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar from the people."

And while he was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he was reclining at table, a woman came with an alabaster flask of ointment of pure nard, very costly. She broke the flask and poured it over his head.
verses.decision_notes: Long Greek participial sentence broken at the natural narrative seam for modern English rhythm.

verses.fhb_text: There were some who said to themselves indignantly, "Why was the ointment wasted like that?
For this ointment could have been sold for more than three hundred denarii and given to the poor." And they scolded her.
But Jesus said, "Leave her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a beautiful thing to me.
For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want, you can do good for them. But you will not always have me.
She has done what she could; she has anointed my body beforehand for burial.
verses.decision_notes: Semicolon preserves the parallel clauses Jesus speaks.

verses.fhb_text: Truly, I tell you, wherever the gospel is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be told in memory of her."
Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went to the chief priests to betray Jesus to them.
verses.decision_notes: Pronoun replaced with 'Jesus' for clarity — multiple male antecedents in the verse (FHB-9.13-1). 'Then' opens the scene shift (FHB-9.3-1).

verses.fhb_text: And when they heard it, they were glad and promised to give him money. So he was seeking an opportunity to betray Jesus.
verses.decision_notes: 'So' for consequential connection (FHB-9.3-1). 'Jesus' replaces second 'him' for clarity (FHB-9.13-1).

verses.fhb_text: And on the first day of Unleavened Bread, when they sacrificed the Passover lamb, his disciples said to him, "Where do you want us to go and prepare for you to eat the Passover?"
verses.decision_notes: Passover lamb preserved literally; the NT names Jesus as the Passover lamb (1 Cor 5:7), which informs the wider arc but is not interpolated here.

verses.fhb_text: And he sent two of his disciples and said to them, "Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him,
and wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, 'The Teacher says, Where is my guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?'
verses.decision_notes: 'Teacher' (Didaskalos) preserved as Jesus's self-naming through the disciples' message.

verses.fhb_text: And he will show you a large upper room furnished and ready; there prepare for us."
And the disciples set out and went to the city and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
And when it was evening, Jesus came with the twelve.
verses.decision_notes: Pronoun replaced with 'Jesus' to anchor the scene (FHB-9.13-1).

verses.fhb_text: And as they were reclining at table and eating, Jesus said, "Truly, I tell you, one of you will betray me, one who is eating with me."
They began to be sorrowful and to say to him one after another, "Is it I?"
He said to them, "It is one of the twelve, one who is dipping bread into the dish with me.
For the Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that man if he had not been born."
verses.decision_notes: 'Son of Man' preserved as Jesus's chosen self-title — Dan 7:13 background, Jesus's most frequent self-reference.

verses.fhb_text: And as they were eating, he took bread, and after blessing it broke it and gave it to them, and said, "Take; this is my body."
verses.decision_notes: Last Supper words preserved verbatim — the canonical institution of communion.

verses.fhb_text: And he took a cup, and when he had given thanks he gave it to them, and they all drank of it.
And he said to them, "This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
verses.decision_notes: Covenant language preserved; the Passover-lamb typology fulfilled in Jesus's blood.

verses.fhb_text: Truly, I tell you, I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of our Father."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: 'Kingdom of God' resolves to 'kingdom of our Father' per FHB-1.2-5 universal resolution.

Renderings:
• "kingdom of our Father" — basileia tou Theou rendered universally as Father's reign per FHB-1.2-5

Greek: basileia tou Theou (kingdom of God) → "kingdom of our Father"
verses.footnote: The kingdom Jesus came to bring is our Father's own reign — the family home he is preparing for his children.

verses.fhb_text: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
And Jesus said to them, "You will all fall away, for it is written, 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.'
verses.decision_notes: OT quotation from Zechariah 13:7 preserved as Jesus delivers it (FHB-1.2-23). Speaker of the quoted line is our Father; Jesus quotes the Father's word about himself as the shepherd struck.

verses.fhb_text: But after I am raised up, I will go before you to Galilee."
Peter said to him, "Even though they all fall away, I will not."
And Jesus said to him, "Truly, I tell you, this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times."
But he said emphatically, "If I must die with you, I will not deny you." And they all said the same.
And they went to a place called Gethsemane. And he said to his disciples, "Sit here while I pray."
verses.decision_notes: Opens the Gethsemane scene; Jesus's prayer to the Father follows.

verses.fhb_text: And he took with him Peter and James and John, and began to be greatly distressed and troubled.
And he said to them, "My soul is very sorrowful, even to death. Remain here and watch."
And going a little farther, he fell on the ground and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
verses.decision_notes: Routine narrative; prepares for the explicit prayer in v.36.

verses.fhb_text: And he said, "Abba, Father, all things are possible for you. Remove this cup from me. Yet not what I will, but what you will."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus's Gethsemane prayer — the canonical Father-Son intimacy moment.

Renderings:
• "Abba, Father" — Aramaic loanword preserved per FHB-1.2-26; scripture itself supplies the gloss (Mark's text gives both)
• "you" / "yours" — Jesus addresses the Father directly in second person per FHB-1.2-1 v4.3 (speaker-context grammatical agreement)

Greek: Abba ho patēr (Aramaic + Greek vocative pair) → "Abba, Father" (preserved verbatim)
verses.footnote: Abba is the Aramaic word Jesus used for our Father — the intimate, child-trusting name. Paul tells us our Father gives this same Spirit-cry to every adopted child (Romans 8:15; Galatians 4:6).

verses.fhb_text: And he came and found them sleeping, and he said to Peter, "Simon, are you asleep? Could you not watch one hour?
Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak."
verses.decision_notes: 'Spirit' here refers to the human spirit (in contrast to flesh), not the Holy Spirit — preserved lowercase.

verses.fhb_text: And again he went away and prayed, saying the same words.
And again he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy, and they did not know what to answer him.
And he came the third time and said to them, "Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? It is enough; the hour has come. The Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
verses.decision_notes: 'Son of Man' preserved as Jesus's self-title.

verses.fhb_text: Rise, let us be going; see, my betrayer is at hand."
And immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas came, one of the twelve, and with him a crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
verses.decision_notes: Mark's 'immediately' (euthus) preserved per Mark's narrative urgency signature.

verses.fhb_text: Now the betrayer had given them a sign, saying, "The one I will kiss is the man. Seize him and lead him away under guard."
And when he came, he went up to Jesus at once and said, "Rabbi!" And he kissed him.
verses.decision_notes:
Renderings:
• "Rabbi" — preserved per FHB-1.2-26 (vocative title in dialogue)
• Pronoun replaced with 'Jesus' for clarity (FHB-9.13-1) — three male antecedents in close proximity

verses.fhb_text: And they laid hands on him and seized him.
But one of those who stood by drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
And Jesus said to them, "Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs to capture me?
Day after day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not seize me. But let the Scriptures be fulfilled."
And they all left him and fled.
And a young man followed him, with nothing but a linen cloth about his body. And they seized him,
but he left the linen cloth and ran away naked.
And they led Jesus to the high priest. And all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes came together.
And Peter had followed him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. And he was sitting with the guards and warming himself at the fire.
verses.decision_notes: Routine narrative; sets up Peter's denial.

verses.fhb_text: Now the chief priests and the whole council were seeking testimony against Jesus to put him to death, but they found none.
verses.decision_notes: 'Now' opens the scene-shift into the trial proper (FHB-9.3-1).

verses.fhb_text: For many bore false witness against him, but their testimony did not agree.
And some stood up and bore false witness against him, saying,
"We heard him say, 'I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another, not made with hands.'"
verses.decision_notes: Quoted false testimony preserved as written; Jesus's actual saying (John 2:19) referenced his body — the false witnesses distort it.

verses.fhb_text: Yet even about this their testimony did not agree.
And the high priest stood up in the midst and asked Jesus, "Have you no answer to make? What is it that these men testify against you?"
But he remained silent and made no answer. Again the high priest asked him, "Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?"
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: High priest's question preserved with reverent Jewish circumlocution.

Renderings:
• "the Son of the Blessed" — eulogētos preserved as the high priest's reverent circumlocution for God per FHB-1.2-1 dialogue preservation (the speaker's wording governs)
• "the Christ" — Christos preserved per FHB-1.2-20

verses.fhb_text: And Jesus said, "I AM, and you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Mighty One, and coming with the clouds of heaven."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus's absolute 'I AM' divine-name claim at trial — the moment that triggered the blasphemy verdict.

Renderings:
• "I AM" — egō eimi rendered in caps per FHB-1.2-6 (absolute claim; v.63 high priest's immediate blasphemy reaction confirms divine-name force)
• "the right hand of the Mighty One" — tēs dynameōs preserved as Jesus's own reverent Jewish circumlocution for the Father in dialogue per FHB-1.2-1 (Jesus deliberately uses the circumlocution before the Sanhedrin); modern English 'the Mighty One' carries the title-form rather than collapsing to 'God'
• "Son of Man" — Jesus's Dan 7:13 self-identification preserved

Greek: egō eimi (I AM, absolute) → "I AM" caps; tēs dynameōs (the Power) → "the Mighty One"
verses.footnote: Jesus answers the high priest with the divine name our Father gave to Moses at the burning bush — 'I AM' (Exodus 3:14). The Sanhedrin understood the claim instantly and condemned him for blasphemy (v.64).

verses.fhb_text: And the high priest tore his garments and said, "What further witnesses do we need?
verses.decision_notes: The high priest's reaction confirms the divine-name force of v.62 'I AM'.

verses.fhb_text: You have heard his blasphemy. What is your decision?" And they all condemned him as deserving death.
And some began to spit on him and to cover his face and to strike him, saying to him, "Prophesy!" And the guards received him with blows.
verses.decision_notes: Routine; preserves the abuse-scene language without softening.

verses.fhb_text: And as Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant girls of the high priest came,
and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, "You also were with the Nazarene, Jesus."
But he denied it, saying, "I neither know nor understand what you mean." And he went out into the gateway, and the rooster crowed.
And the servant girl saw him and began again to say to the bystanders, "This man is one of them."
But again he denied it. And after a little while the bystanders again said to Peter, "Certainly you are one of them, for you are a Galilean."
But he began to invoke a curse on himself and to swear, "I do not know this man of whom you speak."
And immediately the rooster crowed a second time. And Peter remembered how Jesus had said to him, "Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times." And he broke down and wept.
verses.decision_notes: Mark's 'immediately' (euthus) preserved. Closes the chapter on Peter's grief — sets up the post-resurrection restoration arc.

chapter_notes: Mark 14 contains four FHB-distinctive moments: (1) v.25 'kingdom of our Father' per FHB-1.2-5; (2) v.27 the Father's word quoted from Zech 13:7 about striking the shepherd, applied to Jesus per FHB-1.2-23; (3) v.36 Jesus's Gethsemane 'Abba, Father' — the canonical Father-Son intimacy prayer with Aramaic loanword preserved per FHB-1.2-26; (4) v.62 Jesus's absolute 'I AM' claim before the Sanhedrin per FHB-1.2-6, paired with his reverent circumlocution 'the Mighty One' (Jesus's own Jewish-pious naming for the Father in this trial dialogue per FHB-1.2-1). Speaker-context governs throughout: Jesus addresses the Father directly in prayer (v.36 'Father... you... you will'); the high priest's 'Son of the Blessed' (v.61) is preserved as the reverent Jewish circumlocution he speaks. Three footnotes total — kingdom_of_our_father (v.25), abba_father_intimacy (v.36), i_am_absolute_claim (v.62) — none of which appear in the canon-state list of already-used categories.
